title: Foundations - Understanding ML & Exploring Data
grading_philosophy: Module 1 focuses on understanding concepts and exploring data (NOT building models yet). Reward depth of exploration, quality of insights, visualization clarity, and effective use of AI assistants.

sessions.title: Understanding Machine Learning
sessions.purpose: Build conceptual foundation of ML - types, pipeline, when to use
sessions.assessment_type: Conceptual understanding through activities and quiz

criteria.description: Creates comprehensive concept map showing ML types and relationships

excellent.description: Clear, visual concept map with ML at center, branches for all 3 types (Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement), supervised split into classification/regression, 3+ examples for each type, shows relationships between concepts

good.description: Complete concept map with all major concepts, 2 examples per type, basic relationships shown

adequate.description: Basic concept map with main types but missing some examples or relationships

poor.description: Incomplete map, missing major concepts, or unclear organization

criteria.description: Correctly identifies ML problem types and justifies reasoning

excellent.description: Correctly classifies 18+ out of 20 problems (90%+) as classification, regression, or not ML with clear reasoning for each

good.description: Correctly classifies 14-17 out of 20 problems (70-85%) with reasoning

poor.description: Fewer than 10 correct or no reasoning provided

criteria.description: Analyzes real-world problems in student's domain for ML suitability

excellent.description: Identifies 5 domain problems, clearly determines ML suitability for each, specifies classification vs regression, lists required data, defines success metrics (e.g., 85% accuracy)

good.description: Analyzes 5 problems with ML assessment, problem type, and basic data requirements

adequate.description: Analyzes 3-4 problems with basic ML suitability assessment

poor.description: Fewer than 3 problems or superficial analysis

sessions.grading_notes: This session tests conceptual understanding, not implementation. Focus on clear thinking about WHEN to use ML and WHICH type. Use quiz results (15 questions, 70% passing) as additional data point.

sessions.title: Working with Data
sessions.purpose: Explore stock data using pandas - understand structure, features, quality

criteria.description: Deep exploration of chosen stock using pandas commands

excellent.description: Answers all 6+ exploration questions correctly using code (highest/lowest price, average volume, biggest jump/drop, conditional counts), code is well-commented, creates comprehensive summary report

good.description: Answers 4-5 exploration questions correctly with working code, basic comments, summary provided

adequate.description: Answers 2-3 questions correctly, code runs but may have minor issues

poor.description: Fewer than 2 questions answered or code does not execute

evidence_required: Answers to all data exploration questions
Proof of 5 years of data loaded

questions_to_answer: Count of days closing above threshold

criteria.description: Compares 5 stocks from different sectors systematically

excellent.description: Compares 5 stocks across 4+ metrics (avg price, total change %, avg volume, volatility), creates clear comparison table, provides 5+ insights about sector performance

good.description: Compares 5 stocks across 3-4 metrics, creates table, provides 3-4 insights

adequate.description: Compares 3-4 stocks or provides 2 metrics, basic insights

poor.description: Fewer than 3 stocks or minimal comparison effort

evidence_required: Comparison table with 5 stocks and multiple metrics

criteria.description: Identifies and documents data quality issues

excellent.description: Checks for missing values, duplicates, and outliers using appropriate code (isnull().sum(), duplicated(), statistical methods), documents findings, proposes handling strategies

good.description: Checks for 2-3 quality issues, documents findings, basic handling approach

adequate.description: Checks for 1 quality issue or minimal documentation

sessions.grading_notes: Focus on exploration depth, not just completing tasks. Reward students who dig deeper, find interesting patterns, and demonstrate pandas proficiency. Stock data should be real (from yfinance) with proper date ranges.

sessions.title: Data Visualization & Patterns
sessions.purpose: Visualize stock data and identify patterns through charts

criteria.description: Creates 5 different chart types and provides insights for each

excellent.description: Creates all 5 charts (line: price over time, bar: volume, histogram: price distribution, scatter: price vs volume, candlestick), charts are well-formatted with titles/labels/grids, provides 2-3 insightful observations per chart

good.description: Creates all 5 charts with proper formatting, provides 1-2 observations per chart

adequate.description: Creates 3-4 charts with basic formatting and minimal insights

poor.description: Fewer than 3 charts or charts lack formatting/insights

evidence_required: Line chart of closing price over time
Bar chart of volume over time
Scatter plot of price vs volume

technical_requirements.chart_formatting: Must include titles, axis labels, and grid

criteria.description: Compares multiple stocks visually using normalized charts

excellent.description: Creates normalized price comparison chart (all start at 100) for 3 stocks from same sector, clearly shows which performed best, creates 3 volume charts, identifies correlation patterns

good.description: Creates comparison chart for 3 stocks, identifies best performer, creates volume charts

adequate.description: Creates basic comparison for 2 stocks, minimal analysis

poor.description: No comparison chart or single stock only

evidence_required: Analysis of which stock performed best

criteria.description: Finds and documents patterns in historical market data

excellent.description: Identifies and marks on chart: 3 uptrends, 3 downtrends, 2 crashes/rallies, 1 sideways period with dates annotated, researches historical context for major movements

good.description: Identifies 4-6 patterns with dates, basic historical context

poor.description: No patterns identified or no documentation

evidence_required: 5 years of S&P 500 or similar index data

sessions.grading_notes: Visualization is a skill students are building. Focus on: Can they create the chart? Is it readable? Do they extract insights? Don't penalize aesthetic choices if chart conveys information clearly.

sessions.title: Mastering AI-Assisted Learning
sessions.purpose: Develop prompt engineering skills and complete end-to-end analysis
sessions.assessment_type: Prompt quality and comprehensive data project

criteria.description: Creates organized library of effective prompts across categories

excellent.description: Creates 15+ prompts (3 per category × 5 categories: data loading, cleaning, visualization, analysis, debugging), tests each prompt with AI, rates effectiveness (1-5), documents what makes good prompts effective

good.description: Creates 12-14 prompts across categories, tests most, includes ratings

adequate.description: Creates 9-11 prompts, basic testing and organization

poor.description: Fewer than 9 prompts or minimal testing/organization

criteria.description: Complete professional analysis from data loading through insights

excellent.description: Complete project covering: (1) 3 years data loaded & verified (2) Summary statistics + missing values check + 3 insights (3) 5 different charts, each tells part of story (4) 3 patterns/insights with supporting visualizations and explanations (5) Professional notebook with markdown explanations for each step (6) Clear conclusions section

good.description: Complete project with all sections present: data loading, exploration (2 insights), 4 charts, 2 patterns, documented notebook, basic conclusions

adequate.description: Partial project: data loaded, basic exploration, 2-3 charts, 1 insight, minimal documentation

poor.description: Incomplete project, missing major sections, or code doesn't run

evidence_required: Complete Colab notebook link or file

section_1_load_data.requirements: 3 years of data, verification of quality, correct date range

section_2_explore.requirements: Summary statistics, missing values check, 3+ insights discovered

section_3_visualize.requirements: 5 different chart types, each with clear purpose

section_4_analyze.requirements: 3 patterns/insights found, supported with visualizations, explained clearly

section_5_document.requirements: Professional notebook, markdown cells explaining steps, clear conclusions section

sessions.grading_notes: This is the capstone project for Module 1. It integrates all skills: pandas, visualization, AI prompting. Grade holistically - looking for complete end-to-end thinking. Professional presentation matters but don't penalize students learning formatting.

required_completions: At least 3 visualizations in final project
bonus_opportunities: Exceptional visualization in final project (+2 points)
Discovers unique insight not in course materials (+2 points)
Helps peer in discussion forum (+1 point)

instructor_guidelines.grading_approach: Exploration-focused, not perfection-focused. Reward depth of thinking, quality of insights, and thoroughness of exploration. Students should NOT build ML models yet - that's Module 2.

common_issues.session_1: Students confuse classification vs regression - provide examples, use quiz to clarify
common_issues.session_2: yfinance API may have rate limits or outages - allow alternative data sources (CSV backups)
common_issues.session_3: First time creating multiple chart types - focus on effort and insights over perfect formatting
common_issues.session_4: End-to-end project seems overwhelming - encourage breaking into smaller steps

time_expectations.struggling_students: 15-18 hours (encourage office hours for data loading issues)

good_work_indicators: Student connects concepts to their domain
revision_needed_indicators: Code doesn't run or has major errors
No evidence of using AI assistants effectively

feedback_guidelines.praise: Highlight specific insights, good questions, thorough exploration
feedback_guidelines.improve: Guide toward deeper analysis, better prompts, clearer visualizations
feedback_guidelines.encourage: Remind that Module 1 is about UNDERSTANDING not BUILDING - mastery comes in Module 2+

alignment_with_quizzes.session_1_quiz: 15 questions on ML concepts, types, pipeline, when to use ML (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_2_quiz: 12 questions on pandas operations, OHLCV data, features vs labels (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_3_quiz: 10 questions on chart types, visualization interpretation, pattern recognition (70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_4_quiz: 12 questions on prompt engineering, iterative prompting, AI-assisted learning (70% passing)

skills_needed_next: Using AI for code generation (from Session 1.4)
datasets_reused: Stock data (will build predictor in Module 3)
Understanding of data structure carries forward
integration_with_module_2.mindset_shift: Module 1: 'I understand data' → Module 2: 'I can build models'
